submission.title: Waiting Time for interview in El Salvador
submission.body: Good morning everyone hope everyone is doing well. My wife is petitioning me we got our ds260/i864 approved last Monday 7/8. I am hearing it can take a month to get an interview, but then I hear thing that in other countries I can take up to 4 years. Any Salvadorans here that can share their experience. Tyia

comments.body: https://www.visajourney.com/timeline/
My last client was DQ April 2024 and his appt was June 2024. He was IR1
Wow that was fast tbh. Did they get the interview after a month so they can attend to it within a month ?

So dq April 2024, interview letter may 2024 (3weeks later), and then interview in June. Everything in less than 60 days from DQ date. My colleagues client was the same. Have no clue if these two applicants got super, super lucky because other Salvadoran clients waited a few months, but no more than 6 months.
Wow that is incredible possibly lucky lol. I’m in Ohio I don’t think Ohio gets(or receives) that many visas to give out. It’s possibly going to be awhile

My spouse was DQ on May 28 and we are still waiting. I was also seeing a month, but we passed that point and now we are just anxiously waiting!! I hardly ever see anything about the Salvadoran embassy here or on visa journey.
Saaame I hardly ever find anything about it tooo. Definitely will vlog it once I’m done with all of so others can reference it. Do you guys have a lawyer ?
Yes we are working with a lawyer.
Got it hopefully everything works out well for you guys and lmk what happens
Thanks you too! I was thinking that with the recent rain in San Salvador last month maybe they got delayed. But hopefully it’s a short wait.
Thanks, what I have been hearing is that for El Salvador it can be from a month to four. I just second doubt and want a more recent experience then what is online already.
I hope it’s closer to a month than four!!!!
I’m going to start praying for you guys case
Thank you! Likewise!
Amen appreciate it
I got QD on march 2022 and my waiver got approved last month. How long you think it can take to get an interview?
From what I have been hearing it can be anywhere from a month to 6
I got DQ end of may and still waiting for my interview date. I was told it would take anywhere from 30-90 days to hear back.
My friend I just got my interview date this morning at 4 am ( through email)
Wow that’s so freaking awesome, how long did he wait and is it in El Salvador ?

We started the process on February 2021 got DQ on march 2022 but my i601A didn’t get approved until last month. Got an email from nvc saying they got my waiver notice on July 3 and today I got my appointment letter.
I’m freaking excited for you !! A buscar vuelos! I hope that it is this fast for me too

In TX
DQ 06.12.24 
PD 04.06.23
Class CR1
Also,  still waiting for IL.
Buenas any update?
No still no :(
got my interview letter today!
How long did it take?
Almost 3 months!
Not an answer for your question, but how long did it take for getting DS260/I-864 approved? we just submitted on 9/10 and I've seen mixed answers about it.  Also from El Salvador btw
Look at the nvc timeframe for that, but for me it took 10 days
Hi, my husband’s 601a just got approved. We’re waiting for the next steps to get an NVC interview. Has anyone completed this journey? Can you give your testimonial on how this process was for you?
I'm still waiting for my interview after all DQ been approved
My husband was DQ as of Feb 2023. We’re still waiting for the interview. Feeling like we’ve been forgotten.
What's the date of being dq
Submit an inquiry asap saying to expedite it because of how long you have waited

submission.title: Interview waiting time IR1 San Salvador Embassy
submission.body:
Does anyone know how long it's taking for the embassy to schedule an appointment for the inmigrant visas? My case for my IR1 Husband was DQ'd on 06/05/2024

Embassy in San Salvador, El Salvador

comments.body: I am still waiting for my husband's IL and he was DQ'd 05/24/2024. It can be from 2-6 months to receive an appointment but I know friends who have been through same process waited no more than two months.
Please let us know when you hear! We are waiting since DQ 5/30/2024
Will do!

I am waiting for Guatemala IL too my dq was on 06/03/24. I’m looking for people from Guatemala here but I can’t find anyone who can offer me information about the waiting time. You are the closest and we are in the same boat.
Good luck
Hopefully we'll have an answer soon! Good luck to you too
My husbands i601a approval letter came last Monday August 12, he received his appointment date email today for October 4th
Were you DQ'd on August 12th or that was when you received your interview letter?
We just got email for his interview for October 21st!
Congratulations! Thank you so much for letting me know!! I hope our letter comes soon.
Yes hopefully you will get it soon since we are only a few days apart!
My partner’s letter just arrived for the same date!!!!
Really! Congratulations as well! I know this has been mentally exhausting but now hopefully God willing everything goes well at interview. We are almost at the end of this long journey.
Yes we are almost there. It truly is such a mentally exhausting process that’s been taking up a lot of my head space and energy!! Happy for you! Best of luck to your husband in the interview!
Congratulations! We also got the email today, ours was scheduled for October 22nd !
Congratulations to you too! We received the email today and it's for the day after yours, on October 22nd. Is the interview going to be at the San Salvador Embassy?
Yes I believe so. Did you see the most recent notice about the Salvadoran embassy changing their interview scheduling system and payment system? I think that might have been the reason for delay.
And congratulations!! Finally
I did, at first I thought it was mostly related to tourist/business visas but I'm sooo glad it worked in our favor
Guatemala DQ 04May24, IL 31July2024, interview September 9. Spouse of LPR category

We were DQ’d about a year and a half ago, while we were still waiting for the i601a waiver to be approved. We got the approval notice on August 12th and then got on email on August 20th stating that his interview is October 4th. His medical exam is scheduled for September 19th

Have you been able to schedule and confirm the medical appointment? We were supposed to receive a call within 72 hours of selecting a date to confirm the appointment but have not heard anything back and its been almost a week now
We haven't been able to schedule yet. We have a lawyer and we won't see her until Thursday. I'm not sure if there is a number you can reach out but hopefully you receive that confirmation soon!
Please update when you get your IL. We are also Guatemala, so hard to find more info. Hoping you get yours soon!

I’m still waiting and today marks 3 months. As soon as I have news I’ll let you know. The wait has me frustrated.
Good luck for you!!
Okay thanks! Wishing lots of good luck, as well! And I completely understand. It’s so hard to wait when it seems so close
Did you get your appointment yet ? Am waiting for Guatemalan interview also DQ on 8/19/24 ? As of today 10/08 nothing yet!
Any updates on your appointment? So hard to find any info on Guatemala waiting times
Still waiting . It’s barely no info from Guatemala. Let you know any update
Definitely update when you can and praying for your case to get an interview soon 🙏
Hey i received the IL this morning for December 9 !!! I don’t even talk with my lawyer yet.

Yay!!! I have tears for you! Congratulations!! So happy for you! 

Thank you for taking the time to update here! I wish all the best and all the good luck!!
Thank you so much!!! I hope you IL come soon my best wishes for you!! Don’t forget to let me know any update on your case :)

submission.title: Medical exam
submission.body:
Hiya! After almost 5 years we finally got an interview! Hang in there, it will be worth it.

Just wondering, we do have a lawyer does she make the medical exam appointment for us or do I make it? She takes forever to answer at times. Thanks in advance!

comments.body: I would do it myself
Do it yourself.
What country?
El Salvador
Thanks!
Thankss!!
Nice so did we. You having your interview in El Salvador ?
Yes, we have to go to the US embassy out there
No problem. Good luck with everything!
Ok yes same here good luck. Did you make medical appointment yet? Have to asked lawyer if both can be in the interview or just the applicant ?
Good luck to you too! Yes my lawyer sent me an email with instructions. It was fairly easy! Only people listed on the interview email will be allowed inside
O for real ok I thought as the husband, I would be allowed. I havent asked are lawyer yet.
I thought it was like recommended for both to be there
Hello how much was the cost of medical exam?
Hi, a little over $800. It's pricey but worth it! We had to go 3 days before medical exam for covid test

Thanks, I have another question maybe you can help me everywhere I see for K1-visa when it comes to Financial every video says is the Form I-134 but the instructions I received from the embassy of which documents I need and what to do next where it says Financial Documents it says I-864 form so I am confused I have seen videos where they I-864 form is for when applying for green card. Did the embassy make a mistake? Help please.
What type of Qs are asked at interview?

Hi, I know this post is a bit old but my cousin will be having her interview in El Salvador after 9 years! 

Since you also had a long wait, did you have to take or submit a new I-864 and updated tax transcripts prior to the interview? Did they ask for those documents?

Congrats to her! 
I did not have to submit a new I-864 but I did need to submit new tax transcripts!
That’s good to know, thank you so much for replying!

hi! also, did you have to re-submit for the police background considering they expire after a certain time. Additionally, idk if I bring along with me updated tax information of petitoner as when I submitted, i gave the ones the year before. Not sure if I must bring most recent taxes of petitioner

did they call you after you booked for medical appointment on doctorvisa? its been few days and no call back lol 

is the green card printed soon after U.S visit?

if you dont mind me asking, any examples of interview questions that may be asked? 

so many questions im sorry, but it would be so helpful!

Hi, you have ti submit the most recent tax year ! He took them into the interview just incase they asked for physical copy! They actually never called.l, we called and soon found out we had to be there for covid test 2-3 days before 🙄.


Green card was recieved 1 month after US re-entry, and after paying the $235 fee. 


Interview was 4 mins long for him, it was basic questions; home life, family, children.


No worries, it's very nerve wracking!!
You are the best thank you! I wonder if covid test must still be taken for this year, your interview was last year ?

Wait, how were you able to call? I thought there was no number to call! Yeah we did the medical exam request on Doctor Visa, but i know it says 2-3 days and they call back but they havent lol

So how did you find out you also needed covid test? Did it matter where to go to covid test? So you didnt get callback confirming exam, and just showed up to the date you registered on doctorvisa?

I called the doctors office , my anxiety wouldn't let me wait more than a week 🤣. 
They told me when I called about the covid test. 
They confirmed the appointment the day before his covid test :/
Wow lol they seem a lil unorganized! Thank you!!
Hi! Did they ever end up calling you back? It’s been a week for me now and they haven’t called and I’ve tried calling a number I found on google but they also don’t respond
They also did a WHATSAPP text btw! I think they took over 9 days to CONFIRM w me via text😂😂 not thru call!

Omg over 9 days?! and through text?! Ive been anxiously waiting for a call but at least its good to know they could potentially reach out through WhatsApp. Did they text you through your personal WhatsApp? we were told we also had to give a salvadorian number so idk if they would be more likely to reach out through that number instead

Yes!! Make sure it was a Salvadoran number!!! +503

Something weird happened on my portal btw: when first registering, the first box to put # only gave me to put U.S # but then as secondary you could put secondary number
I believe it was the same on our end too. It’s just confusing what number they will call, whether the US one or Salvadoran 😭
I know but its okay be patient! We got it through so you will too :-) !!! Any questions u can ask me!
Thank you so much!

submission.title: Entering the US - Approved green card
submission.body: What line do I take at customs when entering the US for the first time after the interview, and having my green card approved? Do we go through country/continent of origin, or is there a special line to go through customs?

comments.body:
When you enter the U.S. for the first time after your green card has been approved, you will need to go through the line for U.S. citizens and permanent residents. There isn’t a special line just for first-time green card holders, but you’ll be processed as a new permanent resident. 

Make sure to have your passport with the immigrant visa and your sealed immigration packet (if given one during your interview) ready to present to the CBP officer. They will guide you through the process, which includes taking your fingerprints, and a photo, and verifying your documentation. Once processed, you’ll officially enter the U.S. as a lawful permanent resident! Safe travels!
Thank you so much for your reply. This is great information!
What was the process like when you exited the US? did they just ask you for your passport and that was it?
perhaps creating a new post with this question can get more attention. I haven't left the US with a green card yet.

submission.title: IR2 visa documentation
submission.body: Does anyone know if you need to take the petitioners birth certificate for an IR2 visa in El Salvador?

comments.body:
If it's available, bring it.
Better to be over prepared
Bring it with you! Specially if you submitted it on NVC documentation

submission.title: IR1 visa interview El Salvador
submission.body: I have my interview next Monday the 16. Do I need to take more documents to the interview or just the envelope they gave me with my passport and other paperwork in it when I went for documentation review?

comments.body: You need to take every document you submitted online to be DQ. Take original copies only.
Did you end up taking just the white envelope? did they ask you for additional stuff?
I took everything I submitted to NVC and USCIS but he only asked for the white envelope ✉️
Oh ok that’s good to know! How did your interview go? do you know how long it takes to get back the passport? I have my interview soon and I’m trying to get an idea of what to expect!
It was good. I got approved and got my passport back 2 days later
It was good I got approved and got my passport back 2 days later
did you pay for premium delivery to get your passport quick?
No, you need to call the cargo express and ask them if your passport is ready for pick up, they don’t deliver. But call them after your visa has been issued
I think they may be using a different company now because it says DHL express. Did they tell you it would take less than 15 days to get it back after your interview was over?
Idk if they use DHL but if they told you they using it just call them before. Yes they told me could take up to 15 days but mine was ready 2 days after the interview.

submission.title: How long to get passport back El Salvador
submission.body: For those who have had their visa interviews in El Salvador how long did it take to get your passport back? I was told it would take less than 2 weeks but I’m hoping it’s gonna take less time

comments.body: Literally 2 days after
did they also tell you it would take less than 2 weeks to get it after the interview? and did you pay extra to get it quicker?

Yeah, they said it would take less than two weeks but I literally got an email a few days after. Also, you can also download the app called UCS and if you input your receipt, you’re gonna be able to see when the visa is finally printed on the passport and no, you are unable to get it sooner by paying extra.
App uscis*
omg ok! that’s so good to hear!! thank you
I’ve heard anything from 2-7 days. My spouse was verbally approved at the interview yesterday and today it says “administrative processing” online
I got approved this morning and it also says administrative processing, hoping that’s not bad
Congratulations! From what I’ve seen on here it is normal…
oh ok! hopefully we hear back about our passports soon. and congratulations to your spouse!
Actually I just checked and it says issued! So that’s exciting
that’s really good news! do you know if it showed as administrative processing the same day of the interview??
I didn’t check so I don’t know :/
